title: What is the difference between load balancing algorithms?
description: Compare Round Robin, Least Connections, Weighted, and Consistent Hashing.
options: Round Robin: rotate sequentially, simple but ignores load. Weighted Round Robin: proportional to server capacity. Least Connections: route to server with fewest active connections, good for varied request durations. Consistent Hashing: map requests to ring positions, minimizes redistribution on server add/remove, ideal for caches and stateful services. IP Hash: session affinity
Only Round Robin works at scale
correct_answer: Round Robin: rotate sequentially, simple but ignores load. Weighted Round Robin: proportional to server capacity. Least Connections: route to server with fewest active connections, good for varied request durations. Consistent Hashing: map requests to ring positions, minimizes redistribution on server add/remove, ideal for caches and stateful services. IP Hash: session affinity

title: What is the CQRS pattern?
options: CQRS separates read (Query) and write (Command) models into different data structures/services. Commands mutate state through domain logic; Queries read from optimized read models (denormalized views). Benefits: independent scaling, optimized read/write stores, eventual consistency. Often paired with Event Sourcing. Complexity trade-off: not suitable for simple CRUD apps
correct_answer: CQRS separates read (Query) and write (Command) models into different data structures/services. Commands mutate state through domain logic; Queries read from optimized read models (denormalized views). Benefits: independent scaling, optimized read/write stores, eventual consistency. Often paired with Event Sourcing. Complexity trade-off: not suitable for simple CRUD apps

title: Implement a thread-safe bounded channel (producer-consumer).
description: Create a bounded channel with blocking put/get operations.
correct_answer:
import threading
from collections import deque

class BoundedChannel:
    def __init__(self, capacity):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.buffer = deque()
        self.lock = threading.Lock()
        self.not_full = threading.Condition(self.lock)
        self.not_empty = threading.Condition(self.lock)
        self.closed = False

    def put(self, item):
        with self.not_full:
            while len(self.buffer) >= self.capacity and not self.closed:
                self.not_full.wait()
            if self.closed:
                raise RuntimeError('Channel closed')
            self.buffer.append(item)
            self.not_empty.notify()

    def get(self):
        with self.not_empty:
            while len(self.buffer) == 0 and not self.closed:
                self.not_empty.wait()
            if len(self.buffer) == 0 and self.closed:
                return None  # sentinel
            item = self.buffer.popleft()
            self.not_full.notify()
            return item

    def close(self):
        with self.lock:
            self.closed = True
            self.not_full.notify_all()
            self.not_empty.notify_all()

title: What is the difference between monolithic and microservices architecture?
options: Monolithic: single deployable unit, shared database, simpler development/debugging, harder to scale independently, technology lock-in. Microservices: independently deployable services, own databases, independent scaling, technology diversity, but adds complexity (network, data consistency, distributed tracing, service discovery). Choose based on team size and system complexity
They're the same with different names
correct_answer: Monolithic: single deployable unit, shared database, simpler development/debugging, harder to scale independently, technology lock-in. Microservices: independently deployable services, own databases, independent scaling, technology diversity, but adds complexity (network, data consistency, distributed tracing, service discovery). Choose based on team size and system complexity

title: What is the event sourcing pattern?
description: Explain event sourcing and its relationship with CQRS.
options: Event sourcing stores all state changes as an immutable sequence of events rather than current state. Current state is derived by replaying events. Benefits: complete audit trail, temporal queries (state at any point), event replay for debugging, natural fit for CQRS read model projections. Challenges: event schema evolution, eventual consistency, storage growth, complexity
correct_answer: Event sourcing stores all state changes as an immutable sequence of events rather than current state. Current state is derived by replaying events. Benefits: complete audit trail, temporal queries (state at any point), event replay for debugging, natural fit for CQRS read model projections. Challenges: event schema evolution, eventual consistency, storage growth, complexity
